user_query:
I'm a marketing specialist who writes about personal branding and networking. I want a small, verifiable content analysis of advice from credible sources to ground my tips. Using the topics and keywords in input/topics.json, please do the following and produce all outputs under the out/ directory in the workspace:

- For each topic in input/topics.json, use a general-purpose web search engine to find at least 2 relevant pages on .edu domains (career centers, communication programs, etc.). Requirements: (a) use a search engine query for discovery (do not navigate directly to known URLs), (b) choose pages that are HTML (exclude PDFs), (c) pages must be publicly accessible without login, and (d) each topic must have pages from at least 2 different .edu domains. Relevancy check: the extracted page text must contain the topic phrase (case-insensitive) at least once.
- Download each chosen page and save the raw HTML to out/raw/pageN.html (N = 1..total_pages). Extract the main textual content (strip nav/boilerplate best you can) and save to out/text/pageN.txt in UTF-8.
- Build an index CSV at out/index.csv with columns: id,page_id,topic,source_url,final_url,domain,html_path,text_path,word_count. Here page_id is the "pageN" identifier you used for the files; domain is the registrable domain (e.g., example.edu); word_count is the total whitespace-delimited token count of the extracted text.
- Compute keyword frequencies case-insensitively, matching whole words for each keyword string provided for a topic. Create out/keyword_stats.csv with columns: topic,keyword,total_occurrences,mean_occurrences_per_page,pages_with_keyword,fraction_pages_with_keyword (0-1). Compute these per topic across its pages.
- Compute per-topic aggregates and save to out/topic_stats.csv with columns: topic,n_pages,total_words,mean_words_per_page,median_words_per_page,unique_domains.
- Also compute global keyword aggregates across all pages from all topics and save to out/global_keyword_stats.csv with columns: keyword,total_occurrences,pages_with_keyword,fraction_pages_with_keyword.
- Write a short plain-text summary at out/summary.md that includes: (1) a one-line count of pages collected, (2) a bullet list of each topic with n_pages and mean_words_per_page, and (3) the top 5 keywords overall by total_occurrences from out/global_keyword_stats.csv.

Notes and constraints:
- Treat the keywords in input/topics.json as the only vocabulary for counting; do not invent new keywords. Match case-insensitively and as whole words (simple token boundary rules are fine). Do not count occurrences in HTML that you didn’t extract to plain text.
- The Internet steps are essential: discovery must be via a search engine, and you must download and extract content from the pages you select.
- Ensure all paths are relative to the workspace. Create any needed directories (out/, out/raw/, out/text/). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
